evals.name: queue-creation-simple
evals.prompt:
Is there anything wrong with this queue setup?

```swift
let processingQueue = DispatchQueue(label: "myQueue")
```
evals.expected_output: Identifies that queue label should use reverse-DNS format (e.g., com.app.processing) for crash logs and debugging.

assertions.text: Notes queue label should use reverse-DNS format for debugging/crash reports
assertions.rationale: Basic skill content on queue labeling

evals.name: queue-creation-medium
evals.expected_output: Identifies: (1) Scattered global() calls lead to thread explosion and no control, (2) WWDC 2017-706 recommends 3-4 subsystem queues, (3) Should create named subsystem queues (networking, persistence, UI-support, media), (4) Use target queue hierarchies to funnel work.

assertions.text: Identifies scattered global() calls cause thread explosion and lack of control
References WWDC 2017-706 or recommends 3-4 subsystem queues
Suggests target queue hierarchies for funneling work
Notes that barriers are silently ignored on global queues
assertions.rationale: Critical GCD pitfall from skill - WITH mentions, WITHOUT does not

evals.name: queue-creation-complex
evals.expected_output: Identifies: (1) Target queue sync cycle deadlock - requestQueue targets rootQueue, so sync from rootQueue to requestQueue is nested sync on same underlying queue, (2) .background QoS inappropriate for user-visible work - may halt in Low Power Mode, (3) Should use .utility or .userInitiated for thumbnails user will see.

assertions.text: Identifies target queue sync cycle deadlock (requestQueue targets rootQueue)
Notes .background QoS inappropriate for user-visible work
Mentions .background may halt in Low Power Mode

evals.name: deadlocks-simple
evals.expected_output: If called from main thread, main.sync deadlocks - main queue is serial, sync blocks waiting for the block to execute on the same queue that's already blocked.

assertions.text: Identifies main.sync from main thread causes deadlock
assertions.rationale: Most common iOS deadlock, basic knowledge

assertions.text: Mentions libdispatch detects this case and crashes with EXC_BAD_INSTRUCTION
assertions.rationale: Skill-specific detail about libdispatch runtime detection

assertions.text: Suggests dispatchPrecondition for early detection of queue violations

evals.name: deadlocks-medium
evals.expected_output: Identifies nested sync on same serial queue - readFromCache holds cacheQueue, calls refreshCache which calls writeToCache which tries to sync on cacheQueue again. Classic Pattern 2 deadlock.

assertions.text: Identifies nested sync on same serial queue deadlock
Traces the call chain: readFromCache → refreshCache → writeToCache → sync deadlock
Suggests dispatchPrecondition(.notOnQueue) to detect nested sync violations during development
assertions.rationale: Skill's proactive debugging pattern - WITH adds this, WITHOUT does not

evals.name: deadlocks-complex
evals.expected_output: Identifies: (1) ABBA lock ordering deadlock - operationFromFeatureX acquires A then B, operationFromFeatureY acquires B then A, (2) group.wait() on main thread will deadlock if any callback needs main queue, (3) results array has data race - multiple callbacks append without synchronization.

assertions.text: Identifies ABBA lock ordering deadlock between queueA and queueB
Identifies group.wait() on main thread risks deadlock
Notes data race on results array (concurrent append)
Recommends defer { group.leave() } pattern to guarantee leave on all code paths
assertions.rationale: Skill's defensive DispatchGroup pattern - WITH uses defer, WITHOUT does not

evals.name: thread-safety-simple
evals.expected_output: This is correct. NSLock properly protects both read and write. Could use lock.withLock{} for cleaner syntax but functionally correct.

assertions.text: Confirms the implementation is thread-safe
Mentions NSLock provides priority donation (via pthread_mutex_t)
assertions.rationale: Skill-specific lock selection detail - WITH mentions, WITHOUT does not

assertions.text: Suggests OSAllocatedUnfairLock as alternative for iOS 16+
assertions.rationale: Skill's modern lock recommendation - WITH provides, WITHOUT does not

evals.name: thread-safety-medium
evals.expected_output: os_unfair_lock as Swift stored property causes memory corruption - Swift can relocate value types in memory. Use OSAllocatedUnfairLock (iOS 16+) or NSLock instead.

assertions.text: Identifies os_unfair_lock as stored property causes memory corruption
Recommends OSAllocatedUnfairLock or NSLock as safe alternatives
References real-world Alamofire bug as example of this os_unfair_lock issue
assertions.rationale: Skill cites Alamofire production bug - WITH references, WITHOUT does not

evals.name: thread-safety-complex
evals.expected_output: Identifies: (1) CRITICAL: Barriers on global queue are SILENTLY IGNORED - no actual synchronization, (2) Must use custom concurrent queue for barriers to work, (3) Each call to global() may return different queue instance - no coordination between calls.

assertions.text: CRITICAL: Identifies barriers on global queue are silently ignored
assertions.rationale: Most insidious GCD bug - no warning, no error

assertions.text: Notes must use custom concurrent queue for barriers to work
Notes global() calls may return different queue instances

evals.name: dispatch-primitives-simple
evals.expected_output: Correct basic usage. Could improve by using defer { group.leave() } to ensure leave is called on all paths.

assertions.text: Confirms basic usage is correct
Suggests defer { group.leave() } pattern for safety

evals.name: dispatch-primitives-medium
evals.expected_output: Identifies: (1) weak self guard returns without calling leave() - causes hang, (2) Extra group.leave() at the end causes EXC_BAD_INSTRUCTION crash (leave called more times than enter), (3) Should use defer { group.leave() } immediately after enter to guarantee balance.

assertions.text: Identifies weak self guard returns without leave() - causes hang
assertions.rationale: Common mistake with weak self + DispatchGroup

assertions.text: Identifies extra group.leave() causes crash (EXC_BAD_INSTRUCTION)
Recommends defer { group.leave() } pattern

evals.name: dispatch-primitives-complex
evals.expected_output: Identifies: (1) Timer event handler captures self strongly - retain cycle/leak, (2) deinit sets timer = nil but doesn't call cancel() - timer continues firing on deallocated object, (3) Barrier on global queue is silently ignored - no synchronization on events, (4) flush() reads events without synchronization - data race with track().

assertions.text: Identifies timer handler creates retain cycle via strong self capture
Identifies deinit must call timer?.cancel() not just set to nil
Identifies barrier on global queue is silently ignored
Notes flush() has data race reading events without synchronization

evals.name: thread-explosion-simple
evals.expected_output: Thread explosion - each blocking Data(contentsOf:) call causes GCD to spawn new thread. 100 URLs = potentially 100 threads = memory and scheduling overhead.

assertions.text: Identifies thread explosion from unbounded async dispatch

evals.name: thread-explosion-medium
evals.expected_output: Identifies: (1) Thread explosion from unbounded global().async, (2) DispatchSemaphore as mutex lacks priority donation - priority inversion risk, (3) Should use OperationQueue with maxConcurrentOperationCount or concurrentPerform for CPU-bound work.

assertions.text: Identifies thread explosion from unbounded async loops
Identifies DispatchSemaphore as mutex lacks priority donation
assertions.rationale: Skill explicitly warns against semaphore as mutex

assertions.text: Recommends OperationQueue with maxConcurrentOperationCount or concurrentPerform

evals.name: thread-explosion-complex
evals.expected_output: Identifies: (1) Thread explosion - 300+ async calls can spawn 300+ threads, (2) .background QoS may be halted entirely in Low Power Mode - inappropriate for any user-visible work, (3) Scattered global() calls instead of consolidated subsystem queues (WWDC 2017-706), (4) Data(contentsOf:) is synchronous blocking call - exacerbates thread pool exhaustion, (5) No concurrency limit - should use OperationQueue with maxConcurrentOperationCount.

assertions.text: Identifies thread explosion from 300+ unbounded async calls
Notes .background QoS may halt entirely in Low Power Mode
References WWDC 2017-706 or scattered global() calls problem
Notes Data(contentsOf:) is blocking and worsens thread explosion
Recommends concurrency limit via OperationQueue or similar

evals.name: operation-queue-simple
evals.expected_output: For synchronous work this is correct - main() blocks until Data(contentsOf:) returns. Could add isCancelled check before starting.

assertions.text: Confirms synchronous Operation usage is correct
Suggests adding isCancelled check

evals.name: operation-queue-medium
evals.expected_output: Identifies: (1) Missing KVO notifications (willChangeValue/didChangeValue) - OperationQueue won't know state changed, (2) State changes not thread-safe, (3) Missing isCancelled check in start().

assertions.text: CRITICAL: Identifies missing KVO notifications (willChangeValue/didChangeValue)
assertions.rationale: Queue observes via KVO - without it, state changes invisible

assertions.text: Identifies state changes need thread-safety (lock or barrier)
Notes missing isCancelled check in start()
Notes NSLock is safe as stored property unlike os_unfair_lock for protecting Operation state
assertions.rationale: Skill's cross-reference to thread-safety knowledge - WITH mentions, WITHOUT does not

evals.name: operation-queue-complex
evals.expected_output: Identifies: (1) CRITICAL: super.start() should never be called - it marks operation as finished immediately, (2) State variables not thread-safe (KVO callbacks and network callback access from different threads), (3) Missing isCancelled check before starting work, (4) Missing maxConcurrentOperationCount - could spawn unlimited network requests, (5) KVO order should be: willChange for isExecuting, change, didChange for isExecuting, then same for isFinished.

assertions.text: CRITICAL: Identifies super.start() should never be called
Identifies state access is not thread-safe
assertions.rationale: Race between KVO and network callback

assertions.text: Notes missing isCancelled check
Notes missing maxConcurrentOperationCount on queue
Overrides cancel() to cancel the in-flight URLSession task
assertions.rationale: Skill's complete Operation lifecycle - WITH adds cancel override, WITHOUT does not

evals.name: data-races-simple
evals.prompt:
Will this crash?

```swift
var results: [String] = []
DispatchQueue.concurrentPerform(iterations: 10) { i in
    results.append("Result \(i)")
}
print(results)
```
evals.expected_output: Yes - data race. Array uses Copy-on-Write which is not thread-safe. Concurrent append corrupts COW buffer. May crash or produce wrong count.

assertions.text: Identifies data race on Array (COW not thread-safe)

evals.name: data-races-medium
evals.expected_output: Identifies: (1) items and isLoading written from background thread (URLSession callback), read from main thread - data race, (2) tableView.reloadData() called from background thread - main thread violation, (3) Should dispatch UI updates to main queue.

assertions.text: Identifies data race on items/isLoading (written from background, read from main)
Identifies tableView.reloadData() called from background thread
assertions.rationale: UI must be on main thread

assertions.text: Recommends dispatching to main queue for UI updates
Suggests dispatchPrecondition(.onQueue(.main)) for verifying main thread at API boundaries
assertions.rationale: Skill's proactive verification pattern - WITH adds, WITHOUT does not

assertions.text: Mentions Combine .receive(on: DispatchQueue.main) as alternative for reactive/MVVM patterns
assertions.rationale: Skill's Combine integration - WITH provides, WITHOUT does not

evals.name: data-races-complex
evals.expected_output: Identifies: (1) viewContext accessed from background queue - Core Data contexts are NOT thread-safe, must use context.perform{}, (2) cachedUsers has data race - written from multiple threads without synchronization, (3) NSManagedObject entities accessed outside their context's queue - undefined behavior, (4) saveUser notification may trigger UI updates from background thread.

assertions.text: Identifies viewContext accessed from wrong queue (must use context.perform{})
assertions.rationale: Core Data thread safety from skill

assertions.text: Identifies cachedUsers data race (concurrent writes)
Notes NSManagedObjects accessed outside their context's queue
assertions.rationale: Core Data rule - never pass objects across threads

assertions.text: Notes notification may trigger UI updates from background

evals.name: debugging-simple
evals.prompt: How do I enable Thread Sanitizer in Xcode?
evals.expected_output: Edit Scheme → Run → Diagnostics → check 'Thread Sanitizer'. Also works for Test scheme.

assertions.text: Explains enabling TSan via Edit Scheme → Diagnostics

evals.name: debugging-medium
evals.expected_output: Use dispatchPrecondition: dispatchPrecondition(condition: .onQueue(.main)) for UI methods, dispatchPrecondition(condition: .notOnQueue(.main)) for background work. Removed in release builds - zero production cost.

assertions.text: Recommends dispatchPrecondition for queue assertions
Shows both .onQueue(.main) and .notOnQueue(.main) predicates
Notes dispatchPrecondition is removed in release builds

evals.name: debugging-complex
evals.prompt:
I see this Thread Sanitizer output. Help me understand what's happening and how to fix it:

```
WARNING: ThreadSanitizer: data race (pid=12345)
  Write of size 8 at 0x7ffeefbff5a0 by thread T2:
    #0 MyApp.ImageCache.store(_:for:) ImageCache.swift:42
    #1 MyApp.NetworkManager.handleResponse(_:) NetworkManager.swift:87

  Previous read of size 8 at 0x7ffeefbff5a0 by main thread:
    #0 MyApp.ImageCache.image(for:) ImageCache.swift:28
    #1 MyApp.ProductCell.configure(with:) ProductCell.swift:15

  Location is heap block of size 64 at 0x7ffeefbff580
```
evals.expected_output: Explains: (1) Two threads accessed same memory - T2 wrote while main thread read, (2) ImageCache.store (write from background NetworkManager callback) racing with ImageCache.image (read from main thread in cell configuration), (3) Fix: Add NSLock or serial queue to protect ImageCache's internal storage, (4) Could also suggest adding dispatchPrecondition at ImageCache API boundaries to catch future violations.

assertions.text: Interprets TSan output - identifies the two threads and operations
assertions.rationale: Reading TSan output is a skill

assertions.text: Traces the call paths - NetworkManager callback vs ProductCell configure
Recommends NSLock or serial queue to protect ImageCache
Suggests dispatchPrecondition at API boundaries
References Kingfisher, SDWebImage, or Alamofire converging on NSLock for thread-safe caches
assertions.rationale: Skill's real-world library reference - WITH mentions convergence, WITHOUT does not
